steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The first sentence says the bags were impractical for carrying goods. The second sentence reveals these impractical bags dominated the market—an unexpected, contradictory outcome. The transition word must signal this opposing relationship.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content:
”However” directly marks a contradiction between the impracticality in the first sentence and the market dominance in the second.
The other three options cannot signal this contrast—”For instance” introduces examples, “Thus” signals logical consequence (reversing the relationship), and “In other words” restates an idea.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
